title: Bidifferential
summary: Specializes the multilinear framework to two inputs, two weights, and bidifferential tangentiality.

mathematicalContent.heading: Mathematical role
mathematicalContent.body: Specializes the multilinear framework to two inputs, two weights, and bidifferential tangentiality.

mathematicalContent.heading: Formal boundary
mathematicalContent.body: This page describes exactly the declarations present in PolyGJMSoperators/Bidifferential.lean; claims not represented by a declaration are not counted as verified.

Definition: bi Weights. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: def biWeights (_CalConf : Calculus Conf) (w₁ w₂ : ℝ) : Fin 2 → ℝ

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Formal proposition: bi Weights zero. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: theorem biWeights_zero (w₁ w₂ : ℝ) : CalConf.biWeights w₁ w₂ 0 = w₁

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Formal proposition: bi Weights one. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: theorem biWeights_one (w₁ w₂ : ℝ) : CalConf.biWeights w₁ w₂ 1 = w₂

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Formal proposition: pair Input zero. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: theorem pairInput_zero (f g : Function Conf) : CalConf.pairInput f g 0 = f

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Formal proposition: pair Input one. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: theorem pairInput_one (f g : Function Conf) : CalConf.pairInput f g 1 = g

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Formal proposition: left Input pair Input. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: theorem leftInput_pairInput (f g : Function Conf) : CalConf.leftInput (CalConf.pairInput f g) = f

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Formal proposition: right Input pair Input. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: theorem rightInput_pairInput (f g : Function Conf) : CalConf.rightInput (CalConf.pairInput f g) = g

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Formal proposition: total Weight bi Weights. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: theorem totalWeight_biWeights (w₁ w₂ : ℝ) : CalConf.totalWeight (CalConf.biWeights w₁ w₂) = w₁ + w₂

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Formal proposition: bi Output Weight eq. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: theorem biOutputWeight_eq (w₁ w₂ : ℝ) (N : ℕ) : CalConf.biOutputWeight w₁ w₂ N = w₁ + w₂ - 2 * (N : ℝ)

references.note: Source for multilinear tangentiality and Laplacian-word constructions.

limitations: The displayed Lean results are checked relative to structures, typeclasses, certificates, or model laws supplied as hypotheses. The module does not by itself derive every interface field from a concrete geometric backend.
